vulnerability.reference: Protein engineers treat joint generative outputs as validated co-designs due to internal consistency, masking physically impossible pairings.
vulnerability.generated: Protein engineers lack methods to detect subtle coordinated errors in jointly generated sequence-structure pairs, relying on model plausibility.

impact.reference: Systematic failure of designed proteins in experimental assays, wasting significant laboratory and computational resources.
impact.generated: Flawed protein designs waste experimental resources and may produce non-functional or hazardous biomolecules.

dose_response.reference: Undetectable distributional biases in joint flow models produce internally consistent but flawed sequence-structure pairs, which in co-design scenarios mask individual errors, leading to systematic experimental failure and wasted resources.
dose_response.generated: Discrete flow models' undetectable distributional biases, when protein engineers rely on them for end-to-end design, trigger an inability to detect coordinated sequence-structure errors, leading to wasted experimental resources and hazardous biomolecules.
